chief_complaint: A 1-month-old female infant presented with recurrent generalised tonic-clonic seizures.
history: The infant had persistent upward gaze and a prolonged postictal phase. Clinical examination revealed macrocephaly and global developmental delay. Laboratory investigations demonstrated profound hypocalcaemia, with an ionised calcium level of 0.6 mmol/L, attributed to primary hypoparathyroidism. Neuroimaging via brain MRI identified an acute subdural haematoma characterised by a 'bat-wing' configuration along with bilateral diffusion restriction. Subsequent genetic analysis confirmed the diagnosis of glutaric aciduria type 1 (GA1).
exam: Vital signs: not provided. Physical examination revealed macrocephaly and global developmental delay.
labs: Ionised calcium level: 0.6 mmol/L. MRI: acute subdural haematoma with 'bat-wing' configuration and bilateral diffusion restriction. Genetic analysis: glutaric aciduria type 1 (GA1).
correct_diagnosis: Primary hypoparathyroidism as a seizure trigger in type 1 glutaric aciduria.
differentials: Primary hypoparathyroidism as a seizure trigger in type 1 glutaric aciduria.
correct_ranking: Primary hypoparathyroidism as a seizure trigger in type 1 glutaric aciduria.
